ruleName:name: # ^ Bulk filter/transform patch on core Item.tsv. Instead of
# ^ naming individual items (that's the row patch ItemPatch.tsv), a bulk_patch SELECTS rows
# ^ with a `where` expression and transforms the matches.
# ^  - Column 1 (ruleName) is a unique label for the rule (not a parent key).
# ^  - where: evaluated per core Item row in the validator sandbox; `row`/`self` is
# ^    the candidate row, and the validator helpers (any, count, all, ...) are in scope.
# ^  - transform cells: a value starting with `=` is an expression evaluated against
# ^    the MATCHED row (self = that row); otherwise it is a literal. These cells are
# ^    NOT evaluated at load (bulk_patch files load with cell-eval disabled) so the
# ^    expression runs at apply time against the target, not against this rule row.
# ^  - a transform column is typed `expression|nil`: an `expression` cell is stored
# ^    raw (an `=` prefix is tolerated, never evaluated at load), and the value is
# ^    re-validated against the PARENT column's type when applied. Empty = skip.
# ^ This rule adds a 100-gold "luxury surcharge" to every Epic-rarity core item.
